2606.20077.title: The Hidden Evolution of Disguised Visual Context inside the VLM
2606.20077.paper_summary: Visual tokens enter Large Language Models (LLMs) as raw, foreign signals. How they are transformed into meaningful representations and interact with the language space depends entirely on the integration architecture. Whether by treating visual tokens as in-context prompts within the input sequence or injecting them directly into the LLM's intermediate layers. A controlled comparison and understanding of how these architectural choices affect visual information and its internal transformation to integrate with the LLM remains underexplored. We provide a fair comparison by evaluating in-context and layer-wise injection VLM integration paradigms under identical training conditions across single image, multi-image, and video benchmarks. In doing so, we uncover a hidden evolution where visual tokens enter the LLM as disguised visual context, raw representations lacking linguistic structure, but are progressively reshaped depending on the integration paradigm, each capturing fundamentally different frequency characteristics of the visual signal. We show that this evolution inside the LLM determines what visual features the VLM can utilize effectively, how visual representations align with the language space, and ultimately how each paradigm performs across different tasks. We further demonstrate that attention allocation alone is insufficient, and that performance is driven by the quality of visual representations at each layer.
2606.20077.paper_summary_zh: 視覺標記作為原始的外部信號進入大型語言模型（LLMs）。它們如何轉化為有意義的表徵並與語言空間互動，完全依賴於整合架構。無論是將視覺標記視為輸入序列中的上下文提示，還是將其直接注入到LLM的中間層中。對於這些架構選擇如何影響視覺信息及其內部轉化以整合到LLM中的控制比較和理解仍然未被充分探討。我們通過在單一圖像、多圖像和視頻基準下，在相同的訓練條件下評估上下文和層級注入的VLM整合範式，提供了一個公平的比較。在此過程中，我們揭示了一種隱藏的演變，視覺標記作為偽裝的視覺上下文進入LLM，這些原始表徵缺乏語言結構，但根據整合範式逐步被重塑，每種範式捕捉到視覺信號的根本不同頻率特徵。我們顯示，這種在LLM內部的演變決定了VLM能有效利用哪些視覺特徵，視覺表徵如何與語言空間對齊，以及最終每種範式在不同任務中的表現。我們進一步證明，僅僅分配注意力是不夠的，性能是由每層的視覺表徵質量驅動的。
2606.20077.author: Wish Suharitdamrong et.al.
2606.20077.authors: Wish Suharitdamrong, Tony Alex, Muhammad Awais, Sara Atito
